hero.name: Richard J. Valeo, CSM
hero.title: Scrum Master · Regulated Delivery & Compliance-Embedded Agile Practice
hero.value: 15+ years embedding within stream-aligned Agile teams to deliver against business outcomes — with direct experience building compliance and risk-review checkpoints directly into sprint workflows in regulated environments, not bolting them on afterward.

the_case.evidence: 15+ years of Scrum Master and Agile Coach roles across Eptura, Crocs, Understood.org, A&E, DigitasLBI/SunTrust, and more

the_case.requirement: 3+ years in financial services (banking, lending, fintech, payments)
the_case.evidence: Direct financial-services tenure is ~8 months (DigitasLBI/SunTrust, 2014) — shorter than the stated ask. Offered honestly, backed by broader and more recent regulated-delivery experience at Crocs (SOX/SOD controls under live audit review)

the_case.requirement: Regulated environments with compliance integrated into delivery
the_case.evidence: Crocs: designed and embedded SOX-compliant Segregation of Duties (SOD) controls directly into Jira sprint workflow for a publicly traded retailer under active change-control review; DigitasLBI/SunTrust: SOX-compliant SAFe program
Daily practitioner across every recent engagement; built executive dashboards, workflow configurations, and SOD controls directly in Jira

the_case.requirement: Demonstrated success coaching teams toward measurable behavioral improvement
the_case.evidence: Eptura: drove a 35%+ increase in delivery efficiency and a 20% reduction in sprint carryover across a ~10-team program through coaching and process standardization

the_case.requirement: Active Scrum Master certification (SAFe Scrum Master or A-CSM preferred)
the_case.evidence: Certified Scrum Master (CSM), Scrum Alliance — current; SAFe-aligned program experience at DigitasLBI/SunTrust and Eptura

the_stack.description: Agile delivery, compliance, and reporting tools used daily or with direct delivery experience

when_things_break.scenario: A compliance or audit requirement threatens to slow delivery to a crawl
when_things_break.response: At Crocs, I designed SOX-compliant Segregation of Duties controls and embedded them directly into our Jira sprint workflow — not a separate compliance gate, but part of the definition of done — so the team stayed fast without creating an audit problem, under live change-control review for a publicly traded retailer.

when_things_break.scenario: A team's sprint commitments keep slipping and no one has diagnosed why
when_things_break.response: At Eptura, I coached ~10 distributed teams on backlog readiness and WIP discipline, using dashboard data to drive a 35%+ increase in delivery efficiency and a 20% reduction in sprint carryover — root cause analysis over guesswork.

when_things_break.scenario: A distributed, multi-country team needs a Scrum Master who can stabilize a program in crisis
when_things_break.response: At Understood.org, I inherited a distressed, multi-year program spanning 12+ countries in its final crucial months and delivered the replatform through QA/UAT with zero critical regressions.

how_i_run_a_room: Compliance and risk-review steps get built into the sprint definition of done, not bolted on as a separate gate
Every ceremony protects team focus from outside disruption — impediments get removed, not just logged
RAID log maintained and reviewed at every stakeholder touchpoint; risks named before they become issues
Retrospectives produce measurable, tangible process changes — not action lists that expire
Pushes back on unrealistic scope or timeline commitments directly, with data to back it up
Clear line between Scrum Master and Product Owner responsibilities — coaching both roles to stay in their lane

sr_pm_partner.before: Compliance and risk review feel like a separate track bolted onto delivery, slowing teams down and creating friction between engineering and audit/compliance stakeholders
sr_pm_partner.after: Risk review embedded directly into the sprint cycle, a team that moves fast without creating audit exposure, and a Scrum Master who can speak both delivery and compliance fluently

the_ramp.day_one: Review current sprint cadence, backlog health, and existing compliance/risk-review checkpoints for the assigned stream-aligned team(s); read OKR documentation to understand what business outcomes delivery is tied to
the_ramp.thirty_days: Own ceremony facilitation for assigned teams; identify where compliance/risk review can be embedded more cleanly into the sprint cycle; establish working relationships with Product Owners and engineering leads; deliver first retrospective-driven process improvement
